tavily.answer: Yemen's Houthi rebels recently killed 16 government troops in an attack on government-aligned forces, marking some of the most violent fighting seen in years. Simultaneously, a cargo ship was attacked off the coast of Yemen in the Red Sea, according to the British military. The Houthis have been at war with the government since 2015, leading to hundreds of thousands of deaths and a major humanitarian crisis. In a positive development, Yemen’s government and Houthi rebels agreed to free over 1,600 detainees in the largest swap during Yemen’s 11-year civil war, a deal signed in Amman, Jordan, after 14 weeks of negotiations.

acled.note: Your ACLED access tier serves data up to 2025-07-08 (~12-month embargo).

roads.answer: The complex crisis in Yemen involves significant road closures, blocked highways, destroyed bridges, checkpoints, and border crossing closures, which have rendered evacuation routes impassable. The recent escalation in tensions has led to the Houthis attempting to block Bab al-Mandeb, threatening to further disrupt global energy markets and international trade. This situation has been exacerbated by military actions targeting Sanaa International Airport and the declaration of a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia by Yemen’s Houthis, which adds to the threat of a massive cut to global oil flows.
